what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML fragment whose dictionary-interned tokens include a zero-length (or NULL) name, so that xmlDictComputeBigKey is invoked with namelen<=0 and takes the true branch (return(0)). The winner needs deep, repeated, well-formed element/attribute structure that survives parsing far enough to repeatedly intern names through the dict, including a degenerate empty name.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds are clean, recursively-structured XML: '<l n=""><b k:f="><b r:f=&' with an explicit empty attribute value n="" and many repeated '<b k:f=' element/attribute frames. This degenerate empty-name token produces namelen<=0 at the dict-hash call. The branch's true side fires only under grimoire (T=22 vs T=0 for the loser), and grimoire invokes xmlDictComputeBigKey far more (W 334 entry-line hits vs L 105).
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds are byte-corrupted, low-structure inputs ('IxlZ...n="1..l v' and runs of 0d/c5/ff filler) that fail to parse into the deep repeated element/attribute nesting needed to intern an empty name; they never take the true side (T=0 F=105) and call the hash function far less, so the namelen<=0 condition is never reached.
mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire carry the same comparison-solving (input-to-state) stage, so the divergence is NOT comparison-solving. The single-technique delta is grimoire structural: its GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators, which generalize concrete byte runs into <GAP> token structures and splice/recurse on them, building and preserving the deep well-formed XML element/attribute nesting (including the empty-name token) that the flat havoc corpus of cmplog cannot assemble or keep coherent.

what_input_feature: A DTD whose ATTLIST declares >4 default-valued attributes for a single element, so xmlAddDefAttrs is re-entered enough times for the same (name,prefix) entry that defaults->nbAttrs reaches the initial maxAttrs of 4 and the true (grow/realloc) side at line 1243 is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-discovered winner seeds carry a compact, repeatable DTD structure (a small <!DOCTYPE ... SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> shell) and Grimoire's structural mutators recursively splice/extend generalized token runs, letting it replicate the attribute-declaration substructure many times; the winner re-enters xmlAddDefAttrs far more often (entry-line 189 vs 60) and takes the blocker true side 30 times while the loser never does.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog mutates bytes (havoc + I2S substitution) but cannot synthesize a single element accumulating 5+ default-attribute declarations; its larger 368-377 byte seeds spend executions elsewhere and the blocker true side stays at T=0, so defaults->nbAttrs never reaches maxAttrs.
mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire share the same input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so that technique cannot be the differentiator; the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural/grammar-free recombination mutators, which repeat the generalized attribute-declaration substructure enough times to push nbAttrs past maxAttrs.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML element bearing a namespace-declaration attribute with the 'xmlns' prefix (e.g. xmlns:f="") whose value decodes to the empty string, so xmlSplitQName yields ns='xmlns' (matching the ns[0..5]=='xmlns'\0 gate at 1234-1236) and the decoded val is empty (val[0]==0 at 1261).
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire's generalization + structural recombination assembles valid XML containing the literal namespace construct: winner seed 2 contains '<b xmlns:f="">', an xmlns-prefixed attribute with an empty quoted value, which drives ns='xmlns' and an empty val into the blocker.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser is stuck on a degenerate self-repeating byte blob '//+'/*:'*ff(' with no element/attribute grammar, so it never produces a parsed xmlns:-prefixed attribute with an empty value and stays on the false side.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus entries into token/gap structures and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on them, synthesizing the multi-token XML namespace-declaration construct (an xmlns:-prefixed attribute with empty value) that cmplog's per-CMP byte substitution cannot build; this is grimoire structural mutation.

what_input_feature: An input that drives xmlHashUpdateEntry3 to a key slot that is already populated (valid != 0), reaching the else-branch insertion-chain walk at lines 687-728. This requires a second DTD/attribute structure that hash-collides with a prior entry, i.e. structurally repeated/recursive markup, not a single linear declaration.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-produced winner seeds are compact (219-235 bytes) and carry a repeated/spliced token structure (the '<?l ?>' fragment plus the duplicated 'dtds/127772.dtd' run appearing twice in the dump), reflecting grimoire's structural recombination of generalized gaps; these reach the else-branch (W-only coverage at lines 687-728) and hit the branch 76 times.
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds are larger linear DTDs (368-377 bytes) with a single well-formed '<?xml version="1.0"?>' prolog; they only ever take the true branch (L 684 T=30 F=0, insert=NULL) and never enter the else-chain, so they never produce a colliding second entry that flips valid==0 to false.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers run input-to-state comparison-solving, so the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural Grimoire mutators (string/recursive replacement and extension). Grimoire splices generalized token/gap structures to synthesize repeated/recursive declarations that collide on the same hash key, exercising the populated-slot else-branch that cmplog's linear comparison-solving and havoc mutations do not assemble.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid, deeply-nested DTD whose name3 string is reproduced verbatim at the right structural position so the dict interns identical pointers; the byte run must be spliced as a coherent token/gap structure, not a random edit.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus DTD bodies into token/gap structures and its Grimoire mutators splice/recurse them, reproducing the well-formed nested DOCTYPE wholesale (resolved 10/10, hitcount 1004, far above any other fuzzer).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but only havoc+token byte-level mutation; it cannot reconstruct the deep nested structure as a unit and stays blocked 8/10 with hitcount 2.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural Grimoire mutators (string/gap recombination) rebuild the deep DTD structure that cmplog's byte-level havoc cannot; the grimoire_structural delta is the enabler.

what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed DTD document (<?xml ?> + <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> + element/attribute body) that drives the parser deep enough to call xmlStringDecodeEntitiesInt with a zero-length string (str==last), so the line-2650 `if (str < last)` test takes its false branch and falls into `c = 0` at line 2653.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds are grimoire-discovered and carry intact XML/DTD scaffolding -- `<?xml version="1.0"?>`, `<!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">`, `<a><b ...="">` -- produced by GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator splicing generalized token/gap structure; an empty attribute value (`=""`) yields a zero-length decode string so str==last and the false (else) branch executes. The winner hits the function 44 times vs the loser's 9 and exercises the false side (line 2650 W: T=6 F=38) where the loser never does (L: T=9 F=0).
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser-blocking seeds reach xmlStringDecodeEntitiesInt only 9 times and every entry has a non-empty decode string, so str<last is always true (line 2650 L: T=9 F=0, never F). cmplog's byte-level havoc + I2S substitution mutates DTD/path literals (e.g. the `127772.xml`/`.dsd` filename bytes) but does not assemble the structural arrangement that produces an empty entity body reaching this function.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator etc.) recombine generalized XML/DTD token structure to construct documents that drive an empty string into the entity decoder; cmplog shares I2S with grimoire, so the single-technique delta is the structural/grammar-free recombination, not comparison-solving.

what_input_feature: The same deep multi-child content model, but reached by splicing/recombining generalised XML/DTD token structures rather than by gradient climbing; the input must still parse two children and re-enter the separator loop on an invalid token.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolved 8/10 with avg hitcount 12 on the true side; the winning seed is a well-formed '<?xml version="1.0"?>\n<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1' skeleton, and GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators recombine such generalised token runs to grow valid nested content models far past where byte-level havoc stalls.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolved only 2/10 and blocked 8/10; its mutators (havoc/token + I2S) mutate at the byte level without generalised structural recombination, so it rarely reproduces a content model deep enough to parse a second child and re-enter the separator loop.
mechanism_attribution: The single-technique delta grimoire vs cmplog (both have I2S) is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural Grimoire mutators: string-based grammar-free recombination of generalised token/gap structures builds the deep DTD content model; cmplog lacks this structural stage, so the structural recombination, not I2S, is the cause.

what_input_feature: An XML document with a DTD that declares default attributes (e.g. via <!DOCTYPE ... SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">) on an element AND an element instance that explicitly specifies one of those same defaulted attributes, so that during attribute-defaulting the defaulted attname/aprefix dict pointers equal an already-stored atts[j]/atts[j+1] pair.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds build the full nested structure: a DOCTYPE referencing an external DTD plus an element with an explicitly-specified attribute (e.g. <a><b f="..."), so a defaulted attribute collides with the explicitly-given one and the true branch is taken (winner hits the branch 18 times vs 0 for the loser).
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds carry a syntactically valid DOCTYPE prologue but never assemble the co-occurrence of a DTD-defaulted attribute together with the same attribute explicitly present on a matching element instance; the loser enters the defaulting dedup loop (L9667 false 38 times) but never satisfies the inner pointer-equality so the true side stays at 0.
mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire run the identical input-to-state comparison-solving stack, so comparison-solving cannot be the divergence cause. The single-technique delta is grimoire structural: its GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators splice and recursively replace generalized token/gap structures to assemble the deep, multi-clause XML+DTD nesting required to make a defaulted attribute coincide with an explicit one — recombination cmplog's byte/havoc mutation cannot reach.

what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed nested XML document (DOCTYPE + nested elements with namespace-bearing attributes) so the SAX2 start-element path repeatedly invokes xmlNewNs for valid non-xml prefixes.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire's generalization + structural splicing recombine generalised token/gap structures into deeply valid XML; its winning seeds show GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator provenance and a branch hitcount of 1959 (vs loser 1), i.e. it manufactures hundreds of valid namespace declarations per execution.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline grimoire builds on) has I2S but no structural recombination, so it cannot assemble the nested well-formed element structure that produces many xmlNewNs calls — it stays blocked in 8/10 trials with hitcount 1.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on those generalised structures, building grammar-valid nested XML that the cmplog baseline (same I2S, no generalization) cannot construct.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document with an element bearing a default namespace declaration `xmlns="..."`; the surrounding DOCTYPE/element scaffolding must be intact enough that the SAX attribute-internal path runs and calls xmlNewNs with NULL prefix.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire generalizes corpus entries into token/gap structures and splices them, so it recombines the `xmlns` attribute and the valid element/DOCTYPE scaffolding wholesale rather than byte-by-byte; the winning seeds were produced by GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator and reach the false branch with an extreme hitcount of 1959 vs cmplog's 1.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but no structural generalization; it can substitute logged operands at matching offsets but cannot assemble or preserve the larger valid-XML structure needed to repeatedly drive the namespaced-attribute SAX path, so it stays blocked (8/10).
mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural mutation (GeneralizationStage + Grimoire token/gap recombination) reassembles the namespaced-attribute structure that cmplog's I2S cannot build, vastly increasing reach of the prefix==NULL call.

what_input_feature: The same program-side condition (a parsed namespace node with non-NULL prefix freed by xmlFreeNs), but reached by structurally recombining XML token fragments — DOCTYPE/SYSTEM/element substructure including a prefixed element like '<b k:9' — into a fresh well-formed document.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 10/10 with an enormous branch hitcount of 1959 (vs cmplog's 1); its winning seeds carry intact XML structure (' ', 'DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"') and its lineage mixes GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator with I2S, so structural splicing of generalized token/gap runs repeatedly reconstructs prefixed-namespace trees.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (which already has I2S, the single-technique delta being grimoire's added generalization + structural mutators) blocks 8/10 with branch hitcount 1; its blocking seeds corrupt the document header bytes rather than recombining valid structural fragments, so it rarely reaches the namespace-free path.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on these generalized token structures, repeatedly producing well-formed XML containing prefixed namespaces — a capability cmplog's havoc+I2S stack lacks.

what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed XML document with elements bearing namespace declarations, reachable by recombining generalized token/gap structures rather than blind byte havoc.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 10/10 with a very high branch hitcount (2199) and its winning seeds are produced by Grimoire structural mutators (seed 2 mutation_op=GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator,GrimoireExtensionMutator) that splice generalized XML fragments containing element/namespace structure, repeatedly reaching the default-namespace nsDef.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline for grimoire) blocks 8/10 with hitcount 2 — without the GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural recombination it does not reliably assemble the nested element structure that holds a default-namespace declaration.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus structural (grammar-free) mutators recombine generalized XML token/gap structures, building the well-formed default-namespace markup that cmplog's havoc+I2S stack assembles only rarely.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document (prolog + DOCTYPE + nested elements) whose element nodes carry namespace prefix declarations (e.g. a prefixed attribute like 'k:f='), so that xmlSearchNs enters the nsDef while-loop at 6181, evaluates a node whose cur->prefix is non-NULL, and falls through line 6185 (false side) because the prefix does not equal the searched nameSpace rather than matching and returning at 6188.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds are deep, well-formed XML with prolog/DOCTYPE and nested tags including a prefixed name ('<a><b<b k:f='), built by Grimoire structural mutators that splice generalized token/gap structures; per-role coverage shows the winner taking the false-then-continue path (line 6184 [W] return, 6191-6201 [W] orig!=node block) and hitting the branch 30x on average.
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds reach the true side instead (line 6188 [L] return at the inner match, and the L-only xmlNewNs/xmlNsWarnMsg path at SAX2.c 1741-1745), and several loser seeds are byte-corrupted prologs (leading 'fa 00 00 00' / 'f9 00 00 00' garbage, repeated '0f' runs, '//+'/*:'*ff' junk) that do not build the rich multi-namespace element tree required to iterate past a match; cmplog lacks the structural recombination to assemble the valid deep document.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator, GrimoireExtensionMutator) recombine generalized XML token/gap structures into deep well-formed documents with multiple namespace-prefixed nodes; cmplog (which shares the same I2S stage) only mutates bytes/tokens and cannot reliably synthesize the nested namespaced tree, so the divergence is attributable to the grimoire-structural technique, not I2S.

what_input_feature: A syntactically intact URI of the form scheme://host:PORT/path where the authority ends in ':' followed by decimal digits, so xmlParse3986 sets uri->port to a positive integer; the colon, host, and digit run must survive as one contiguous, uncorrupted token.
why_winner_satisfies: The grimoire winner seed holds a clean, duplicated URL block 'http://3.org:1999/n' twice, with the ':1999' port token intact; the winner is the only set that hits the true side of L1178 (W T=1 F=0 vs L T=0 F=24) and executes the port-printing snprintf at L1184.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser seeds carry garbled authorities ('http://www.k' with byte d1, 'http://www.w3.o' with a backslash for slash, embedded NUL runs '06 00 00 00') so a numeric port never parses; the loser only ever reaches the false side of L1178 (L T=0 F=24).
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so the one-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural/grammar-free Grimoire mutators: generalization carves the URL into reusable token/gap structure and the Grimoire splice/recursion mutators recombine whole well-formed authority tokens (hence the cleanly duplicated 'http://3.org:1999/n' block), preserving the colon+digit port run that cmplog's byte-level havoc mutations corrupt.

what_input_feature: A DOCTYPE SYSTEM declaration whose systemId, after URI-building setup, yields an empty URI string at xmlBuildURI so *URI==0 and the false arm (ret=0) executes; this requires degenerate/generalized SYSTEM-id bytes rather than a normal concrete filename like a non-empty "dtds/1..." path.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry generalized/spliced structural mutations (lineage shows GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator,GrimoireExtensionMutator,GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator) producing degenerate URI fields (leading 27 ' quote, repeated %%% and ///////// runs) that collapse the systemId to an empty string, so the branch resolves on all 10/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are stuck on a fixed concrete structure beginning 06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 37 32 2e 78 6d 6c ("127772.xml") with a non-empty "dtds/1..." SYSTEM path, which always yields a non-empty URI so *URI!=0 (true branch) and the false side stays blocked 8/10 trials; cmplog's havoc+I2S mutations cannot restructure this into the empty-systemId shape.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the same input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so it is not the delta; the winner is enabled by grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus structural Grimoire mutators (GrimoireExtension/RecursiveReplacement/RandomDelete) that splice generalized token/gap structures into the degenerate SYSTEM-id shape cmplog never reaches.

what_input_feature: A document whose external-entity systemId resolves to an empty URI string: the byte handed to xmlBuildURI as URI begins with a NUL so *URI==0 at line 1921, skipping xmlCreateURI() and leaving ref==NULL. The reference must be empty while a non-empty base is still parsed (so ret==0 at 1947 and control reaches line 1952 with ref==NULL).
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry NUL bytes inside the URI/DOCTYPE region (e.g. seed 3 byte 0x0b = 00, seed 5 byte 0x0c = 00) produced by structural splice/delete of generalized token runs; at d=1 line 1921 the winner takes *URI false 30 times (T=0 F=30) and consequently L1952 true (T=11), executing the empty-reference body at lines 1956-1962.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are well-formed DOCTYPE documents with a non-empty SYSTEM literal ('dtds/1...', '127772.xml'); at d=1 line 1921 the loser takes *URI true (T=30 F=0), so xmlCreateURI() runs, ref is non-NULL, and line 1952 is always false (T=0 F=20) — the true branch is never reached, blocking for 20.80h.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and its structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator/GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) splice and delete whole token/gap segments, which can collapse the systemId field to empty (leading NUL) while keeping the surrounding DOCTYPE grammar valid. Plain cmplog I2S only substitutes logged CMP operands at byte matches and cannot manufacture this structural emptiness, so it stays stuck on non-empty literals.

what_input_feature: A structurally varied XML document whose DOCTYPE/SYSTEM external-entity URIs reach xmlBuildURI along paths where ref ends up NULL (empty/degenerate reference URI) so the cleanup test if(ref!=NULL) takes its false side; the winner exercises this branch heavily (hitcount ~861) by generating many distinct URI/DOCTYPE structures, not just one fixed dtds path.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-resolved seeds carry diverse generalised URI+DOCTYPE structures (e.g. 'http://www.w...' followed by '<?l?><!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "">' and degenerate forms like 'http://#.//0=//') that drive xmlBuildURI into the empty-/null-ref code path; winner resolved 10/10 with branch hitcount 861 and the d=1 L1921 split shows the winner taking the empty-URI false side (F=30).
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog seeds are pinned to a single near-fixed template '127772.xml ... <?xml version="1.0"?> ... <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' across all blocking seeds, so it keeps re-parsing the same systemId shape and stays on the ref!=NULL true side; loser resolved only 2/10, blocked 8, branch hitcount 1.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the full cmplog/I2S stack, so I2S substitution cannot be the discriminator; the winning technique is grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators, which recombine generalised token/gap structures to synthesize the structurally varied DOCTYPE/SYSTEM-URI documents that reach the ref==NULL false side, whereas cmplog's havoc+I2S cannot restructure the fixed dtds template.

what_input_feature: Same program condition: a DTD <!ELEMENT> content model with a multi-child SEQ/OR group whose freed node maps to parent->c2, but reached via structural recombination of generalised DTD token fragments rather than gradient byte search.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves the false branch in 8/10 trials with the highest hitcount on the branch (63), and its GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators splice generalised token/gap structures, letting it recombine '<!ELEMENT name (a|b|...)' fragments into deeply nested multi-alternative content models that create the c2 sibling driving line 1128.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline grimoire builds on) blocks 9/10 with hitcount 0; it lacks the generalization + structural recombination, so it cannot assemble the multi-child group and never produces a parent->c2 node to free.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators (string-based, grammar-free recombination of generalised token/gap structures) build the deeply nested multi-alternative '(a|b|...)' content model that cmplog cannot, producing a parent->c2 child and the false branch at valid.c:1125.

what_input_feature: A well-formed external-subset DTD that declares an element with at least two attributes, where the first attribute already inserted into elemDef->attributes is named (or prefixed) "xmlns", so that when a second ATTLIST is added the while-loop at line 2142 finds a non-NULL tmp whose name equals BAD_CAST "xmlns" and takes the true side of line 2143. This requires deep, repeatedly-valid structural nesting: <!DOCTYPE> with SYSTEM dtd, an element, and multiple attribute declarations sharing an element, one being an xmlns namespace declaration.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds preserve the full valid skeleton (UTF8 header, <?l ?>, <!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">, <a>, nested <b k:...> children) while recombining generalized token/gap structures, producing inputs with multiple namespaced attribute declarations on one element. The branch reaches hitcount 76 and resolves in all 10 grimoire trials, and grimoire-discovered seeds carry GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireExtensionMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator ops that splice these structural fragments.
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds keep a flat, shallow layout (a binary length prefix 06 00 00 00, then 127772.xml, then a single <?xml ...?><!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM ...> body) and are byte/word havoc + I2S mutations. They reach the blocker far less (hitcount 2, blocked in 8/10 trials) and on line 2143 take F=47 vs T=6, never building the second-attribute-with-prior-xmlns structure that drives the true side; I2S substitution alone cannot synthesize the multi-attribute nesting.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts the valid DTD/XML byte runs into <GAP>-templated tokens, and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator/ExtensionMutator) recombine those tokens to emit DTDs with multiple attribute declarations including an xmlns one on a shared element. Plain cmplog (havoc + I2S only) cannot perform grammar-free structural recombination, so it rarely constructs the prior-xmlns-entry list that the line-2143 loop scans.

what_input_feature: An input that drives the SAX parser through xmlSAX2AttributeDecl into xmlAddAttributeDecl on the external subset (ctxt->inSubset==2) at a point where the DTD's child list is still empty, so the dtd->last==NULL true branch fires (line 2164 -> 2165). This requires assembling a well-formed XML document with a DOCTYPE/external-subset declaration plus a syntactically valid first <!ATTLIST> declaration, including varied element/attribute structure (e.g. xlink attributes, alternate encodings, nested elements) that the cmplog fixed template never produces.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry diverse, structurally recombined XML: ISO-8859-5 encoding decls, nested '<a><b xlink="">' elements, varied DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtds references, and processing-instruction variants. The W role hits xmlCheckDefaultedAttributes (44 vs 0), xmlSAX2StartElement (44 vs 0) and xmlSAX2AttributeInternal (28 vs 0), and reaches line 2165 (dtd->children=dtd->last=ret), confirming the first-declaration true branch executes on the external subset. Branch L2164 shows W T=27 F=9 vs L T=0 F=66 — the winner takes the true side 27 times while the loser never does.
why_loser_doesnt: All five cmplog loser seeds are near-identical fixed boilerplate ('<?xml version="1.0"?>' + '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772...') sharing the same 06 00 00 00 prefix and only trivial byte-level mutation (DwordInteresting, ByteDec/ByteAdd, BytesSwap). cmplog's I2S + havoc cannot synthesize the structural variation needed to enter the external subset with an empty child list, so L2164 is taken false 66 times and never true; the loser instead routes through xmlSAX2ElementDecl/xmlAddElementDecl (0 vs 57 in W) and xmlValidateAttributeDecl (0 vs 9), a different declaration-handling path that leaves dtd->last already populated.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus XML into <GAP> token structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireExtensionMutator, GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) splice/recurse on those grammar-free token structures, generating the well-formed-yet-novel DTD/ATTLIST arrangements that reach xmlAddAttributeDecl on a fresh external subset. cmplog has the same I2S stage but lacks generalization + structural recombination, so it stays stuck on byte-level edits of one template.

what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed XML document: a parseable `<?xml ...?>` prolog, a `<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...dtd">` declaration that resolves an internal/external subset, and an element with a namespace-prefixed attribute (e.g. `<b k:f="">`) so that elem!=NULL reaches valid.c:2791 and constructs fullelemname via xmlBuildQName.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry intact grammar tokens: `<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">` followed by `<a> <b k:f="">`, a prefixed attribute name that drives ns/prefix-bearing element processing all the way through SAX2 into xmlIsID's else-arm (line 2791 True side hit 8 times by W).
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds have corrupted/truncated DTD references (`dtdskk`, `dtds;1`, `dtds/1`-truncated) or a mangled prolog (`eeeeeTTLIST`), so the document is not well-formed or the DTD does not resolve; xmlIsID is reached but only its early-return paths execute (line 2791 True never taken, only the no-prefix False side at hit-count 39 vs 8).
mechanism_attribution: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts the corpus XML into token/gap structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireExtensionMutator etc.) splice those generalised grammar fragments to assemble a still-well-formed document with a resolvable DTD plus a prefixed element/attribute; plain cmplog (havoc+I2S, which grimoire also has) cannot maintain whole-document grammar validity across mutations, so the single-technique delta is grimoire structural mutation, not I2S.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML prolog followed by `<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...dtd">` and a first attribute declaration, so xmlAddAttributeDecl calls xmlGetDtdElementDesc2 on a DTD whose element table is still NULL (taking the blocked true side at valid.c:3286 and creating the table).
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry intact structural tokens — `<?l ?><!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">` and `<a><b xlink="">` — assembled by Grimoire splice/recurse mutators that preserve grammar-shaped token runs, so the parser reaches the DTD/attribute path and hits the table-creation true branch (W: T=27 F=9 at L3286, avg hitcount 286).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds keep a corrupted prolog region (`06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 37 32 2e 78 6d 6c` / `=====27772.`) ahead of the `<?xml` token and never establish the fresh-DTD-then-attribute sequence that first populates the element table, so they take the false side (L: T=0 F=66 at L3286) and stay blocked 19.5h on average.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus bytes into <GAP> token structures and the Grimoire mutators (Extension/RecursiveReplacement/RandomDelete) splice grammar-shaped fragments, building the valid DOCTYPE+attribute layout that bare cmplog (havoc + token + input-to-state byte substitution, no structural recombination) cannot assemble, even though both fuzzers run the same i2s stage.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML DTD/attribute construct whose Name token contains a character matched at valid.c:3555 (c == '_' or c == ':'), reached only after the input parses into a real DOCTYPE/attribute-decl structure rather than a flat byte run.
why_winner_satisfies: The grimoire winner seed is a structured XML document with a real DOCTYPE declaration (<!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">); grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators recombine generalised token/gap structures so name-token characters flow into xmlIsDocNameChar, and the True side of L3555 is actually taken (T=60).
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser seeds are dominated by long single-byte runs (offset-concentrated 0x37('7')x38 etc.) from havoc/I2S byte substitution; they never synthesize a Name token that satisfies '_'/':' so L3555 stays False (T=0 F=161) and the branch blocks.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural mutation is the winning edge: the GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus entries into token/gap structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (extension/recursive-replacement/string-replacement) splice grammar-free structural fragments, building the syntactically valid Name token that drives the attribute-decl validation path; cmplog's comparison-operand substitution can only swap logged CMP bytes at matching offsets and cannot construct the surrounding grammatical structure, so it cannot present a valid Name char to this gate.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document carrying a DOCTYPE with a SYSTEM-referenced DTD plus element/attribute markup (e.g. <!DOCTYPE...SYSTEM "dtds/...dtd">...<a><b xlink:f="">), so an attribute declaration's value is parsed and routed into xmlValidateNmtokens/Name/AttributeValueInternal, calling xmlIsDocNameChar with a real name character on the doc->properties XML_DOC_OLD10 path.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds are short, well-formed XML where a clean <?l ?> declaration, <!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> and concrete element/attribute markup <a><b xlink:f=""> reach the validation call chain; the generalization+structural-recombination stage assembles and preserves this nested grammar structure so the attribute value feeds xmlIsDocNameChar with the true branch taken (W shows L3573 T=123).
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds carry a leading filename/garbage prefix (e.g. 127772.xml\n or 06 00 06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 37 32 ...) before the <?xml version="1.0"?> declaration, so the document is malformed earlier and the attribute value path that reaches the true side fires far less often (L shows L3573 T=30); havoc/I2S byte substitution does not reliably build the nested DOCTYPE+element grammar that grimoire structural mutation does.
mechanism_attribution: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireExtensionMutator) splice/recurse on those generalized token structures, enabling grammar-free assembly of the nested DOCTYPE+element document that cmplog (I2S byte substitution only) cannot synthesize.

what_input_feature: The same NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute context, but reached by structurally recombining generalised DTD/element token fragments so the attribute value begins with a non-NameChar; grimoire reaches it far faster (avg blocked 0.10h, hitcount 333) than cmplog.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire winner seeds carry Grimoire structural mutators (mutation_op=GrimoireExtensionMutator, GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) and are compact restructured DTD inputs; grimoire resolves 10/10 with avg duration blocked 0.10h and hitcount 333, indicating it rapidly assembles the valid DTD+ATTLIST skeleton and varies the attribute-value head structurally.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser stays blocked 8/10 at avg duration 19.10h with hitcount 2; it has the cmplog I2S stage but lacks the GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators, so it cannot recombine the DTD token structure to land a non-NameChar leading attribute value within budget.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire builds on the full cmplog stack (so I2S is held constant between grimoire and cmplog) and ADDS a GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators that splice generalised token/gap structures; this grammar-free structural recombination of the DTD/element skeleton is the single-technique delta (grimoire_structural) that produces the qualifying attribute value.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document whose validated element carries a resolved namespace WITH a prefix: a 'prefix:localname' element name plus a matching 'xmlns:prefix' declaration so that the DTD-validation path computes elem->ns != NULL and elem->ns->prefix != NULL at valid.c:4071. This requires composing several co-dependent structural fragments (DOCTYPE/DTD with an ATTLIST, a namespace-declaring attribute, and a prefixed element) that must all be present and consistent in one input.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire generalizes corpus entries into <GAP> token structures and splices them, so it can recombine a known xmlns/prefix fragment with a prefixed-element fragment into one consistent document; the winning seed shows the assembled scaffold (xml prolog + DOCTYPE a SYSTEM 'dtds/127772.dtd') and grimoire resolves the branch in 8/10 trials with 414 avg hits on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S substitution and havoc/token mutation but no structural generalization/recombination, so it can solve individual byte comparisons but rarely assembles the multi-fragment prefixed-namespace structure; it blocks 8/10 trials with only 1 avg hit, and its loser seeds reach valid.c:4071 but always take the false side (T=0 F=33).
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers run the same input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so comparison-solving is not the delta; grimoire's single-technique advantage is the GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators (string-based, grammar-free recombination of generalised token/gap structures), which build the consistent prefixed-namespace document the gate at line 4071 needs.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML+DTD where a <!ELEMENT ...> declaration drives xmlAddElementDecl to return NULL (e.g. a redeclared/duplicate or otherwise rejected element decl), so the SAX2 caller sets elem=NULL and then calls xmlValidateElementDecl with that NULL elem, exercising the true side of `if (elem == NULL) return(1)`.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-discovered winning seeds build structurally complete DTD declarations (DOCTYPE + element-name token runs like 'atttttttttt', repeated 'dtds/127772.dtd' paths); the per-role overlay shows the winner takes the NULL-elem path 21 times (W T=21) at both the caller's `if (elem == NULL)` and the blocker line, reaching `ctxt->valid = 0` (line 801, [W]-only).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds parse as a single valid <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM ...> declaration whose element decl succeeds, so xmlAddElementDecl never returns NULL; the loser takes the false side 143 times (L T=0 F=143) and instead descends into the MIXED-content body and redefinition checks (line 4324 L T=60), never producing a NULL elem.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and its structural mutators (GrimoireExtension/RecursiveReplacement/StringReplacement) splice and recurse on those generalised DTD token structures, letting it assemble the multi-declaration / redeclaration shape that makes xmlAddElementDecl return NULL — a structural recombination capability cmplog (havoc + I2S only, both fuzzers share I2S) lacks.

what_input_feature: A valid XML document whose DOM produces, while xmlTextReaderRead traverses forward from an XML_TEXTREADER_ELEMENT state, a node that is an XML_ELEMENT_NODE with a non-NULL next sibling (reader->node->next != NULL at L1326) AND whose type is XML_ELEMENT_NODE at L1328. The branch needs the tree to actually be built into element nodes with sibling structure, not merely a syntactically present XML header.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire wins 10/10 and is the only fuzzer to enter the L1328 true side at all (W shows T=1 F=3 vs L T=0 F=20). The GeneralizationStage abstracts byte runs of a coverage-preserving XML corpus entry into <GAP> tokens and the Grimoire string/structural mutators recombine those generalized fragments, so it can synthesize new well-formed element/sibling tree shapes that drive reader->node to an element node with a following sibling, taking the true branch and reaching L1335-1337 (W-exclusive lines).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog reaches L1327 (node->next != NULL) but never satisfies L1328 (L: T=0 F=20) — its seeds are mutated XML text (valid <?xml version="1.0"?> headers, DOCTYPE) but the resulting parse tree does not present an XML_ELEMENT_NODE with a following sibling at the traversal point; cmplog's byte-level havoc + I2S + token mutators perturb literals/bytes but do not construct the required sibling-element grammar structure, so it falls through to the L1370/L1384 backtrack path (L-exclusive lines).
mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the single-technique delta over cmplog is grimoire's GeneralizationStage (<GAP> abstraction) plus the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireExtension/RecursiveReplacement/StringReplacement) that splice generalized token/gap structures. This grammar-free structural recombination builds new well-formed XML element/sibling shapes that cmplog's byte/I2S/token mutators cannot. This is structural-mutation attribution, not comparison-logging substitution: both fuzzers already run the same input-to-state stage, so I2S cannot be the divergence axis.

what_input_feature: A syntactically valid XML element carrying an attribute whose value parses to an XML_ATTRIBUTE_NODE with an empty (children==NULL) value, e.g. element-open + attribute-name + quoted value markup such as `<l n=""><b ...>`, so the reader loop reaches xmlTextReaderReadAttributeValue on a childless attribute node.
why_winner_satisfies: The grimoire winner seeds carry intact XML element/attribute markup produced by structural splicing — `<l n=""><b ...>` appears at offset 0x10 onward, and lineage shows the leaf was built by GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator recombining generalised token/gap structures rather than mangling raw bytes. The branch true side is taken 25 times (avg hitcount=25) and resolved in 10/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser seeds are byte/token mutations of a fixed `<?xml version="1.0"?> <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...` skeleton (offsets 0x10-0x40 are DOCTYPE boilerplate), so the parser stays in DOCTYPE/external-entity territory and the attribute-node loop never produces a childless XML_ATTRIBUTE_NODE: at the blocker cmplog shows T=0 F=11 and blocked 9/10 trials for 23.30h.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators: generalising corpus bytes into token/gap templates and recursively splicing them lets grimoire synthesise the nested element-with-attribute markup that constructs a childless attribute node, which per-offset byte substitution alone (cmplog) does not assemble.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document with nested element open/close tags (e.g. ) so the reader, after descending into an element, reaches reader->state == XML_TEXTREADER_END or XML_TEXTREADER_BACKTRACK while node->type is XML_ELEMENT_NODE, hitting line 2952's true side.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry intact XML grammar fragments — DOCTYPE/SYSTEM declarations plus element bodies like '><a><b f=""><' — produced by recursive structural splicing of generalized token/gap structures, so the reader walks far enough to enter the end/backtrack state and the branch true count is non-zero (W T=3 at L2952).
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds stall in the early document prolog/parse loop; at the blocker line their true count is zero (L T=0 F=23) and they never reach an element-end state, because byte-level I2S/havoc/token mutation does not synthesize the balanced nested open/close element structure required to advance the reader to XML_TEXTREADER_END/BACKTRACK.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus entries into <GAP>/token structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (RecursiveReplacement, RandomDelete) splice grammar-shaped fragments, generating well-nested element documents that cmplog's byte-oriented havoc+token+i2s stack cannot assemble; since the input-to-state stage is present in both fuzzers, the divergence is attributable solely to grimoire's structural mutation layer.

what_input_feature: Same program-side condition (a default namespace declaration so cur->prefix==NULL at line 603), but reached by structural recombination: a generalized XML token/gap structure spliced to place a bare xmlns (no prefix) on an element.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves the false side in 10/10 trials with a very high branch hitcount (625), consistent with its GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators repeatedly splicing generalized markup tokens into many valid default-namespace declarations that all flow into xmlNsDumpOutput with prefix==NULL.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline for this pair, sharing I2S but lacking generalization + Grimoire mutators) stays blocked 8/10 with branch hitcount 0; its byte-level havoc+I2S mutations do not assemble the structural default-namespace element needed to take the false branch.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on these generalized token structures, building syntactically valid XML that carries a prefix-less (default) namespace declaration, which byte-level cmplog mutation does not reach.

what_input_feature: XML containing an element that is bound to a namespace WITH a prefix and is fully serialized (open + children-or-empty + close), so the writer reaches the closing-tag emit at line 1050 with cur->ns->prefix != NULL. This needs a valid prefix:localname construction plus a matching xmlns:prefix declaration, e.g. the grimoire winner's '<b k:f="">' style prefixed token inside a DOCTYPE/element structure.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire's generalization + structural string mutators recombine the generalized DOCTYPE/element token structure to keep emitting syntactically valid prefixed elements: winner seed 1 contains the prefixed construct '<b k:f="">' and the per-role overlay shows grimoire (W) actually takes the true side of the namespace-prefix check at the opening tag (line 872 W=T=7) and reaches the closing-tag blocker with W resolving 8/10 trials and hitcount 606 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog (havoc + I2S, no structural generalization) almost never assembles a namespaced element: at the opening-tag namespace-prefix check line 872 the loser shows T=0 F=73 (zero true), so cur->ns->prefix is never non-NULL, and the closing-tag blocker line 1050 shows L T=0 with the loser blocked 8/10 trials and hitcount 0 on the branch.
mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire share the input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so the divergence is NOT comparison-solving; the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural string/gap mutators, which preserve and recombine the generalized XML token structure to repeatedly produce a valid prefixed namespaced element that cmplog's byte-level havoc cannot reliably build.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document declaring an element with a non-CDATA (tokenized) attribute via DOCTYPE/DTD, where an attribute instance carries a value containing a 0x20 space *interior* to the value (a space after at least one non-space character) so xmlValidNormalizeString's while(*src!=0) loop reaches a space and takes the if(*src==0x20) true branch at valid.c:2607.
why_winner_satisfies: The grimoire winning seed is a structurally complete XML doc (`<?xml...><!DOCTYPE...SYSTEM "dtds/..."><a><b xlink:hr'f=...`) reaching the normalizer 14 times and crossing the blocker (W: L2607 T=3), with grimoire's generalization/structural mutators able to splice space-separated attribute tokens into a valid DTD-validated attribute value, satisfying the non-CDATA + interior-space requirement.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocked on all 10 trials (loser blocked=10, avg hitcount on branch=0); its seeds reach the normalizer (L: L2606 T=135) but never the interior-space branch (L: L2607 T=0 F=135) — its havoc/I2S mutations reproduce the parse structure and even the same DTD literals but never produce an attribute value with an internal space inside a non-CDATA attribute, so every entry into the loop is a non-space character routed through the else arm.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts byte runs into <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire structural mutators (extension/recursive/string-replacement) recombine these generalized token structures grammar-free, letting it construct a syntactically valid multi-token (space-containing) attribute value in a DTD-tokenized attribute; plain cmplog (havoc+token+I2S) lacks this structural recombination and cannot assemble the required interior-space attribute, so the divergence is attributable to grimoire structural mutation.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document with nested element nodes that, during streaming reader traversal, lands on an XML_ELEMENT_NODE having no children and no NODE_IS_EMPTY flag, while the previous state was XML_TEXTREADER_ELEMENT and node->next is NULL (sibling list exhausted). This requires the parser to build a real element tree deep enough to back-track over a terminal childless element.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire winner seeds carry coherent element grammar: seed 1 holds `<!DOCTYPEa SYSTE`/`td"><a><b f=""><` (nested <a><b> elements with an empty attribute), so the reader walks into element children and exhausts siblings, driving the true branch at L1371 — its hitcount on the branch is 4 vs the loser's 0, and DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows W reaches L1371 T=2 while L is T=0.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are byte-mangled length-prefixed blobs (`06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 37 32 .xml`) that never assemble the nested element structure; at the blocker line L records T=0 F=14, meaning the reader repeatedly evaluates the predicate but node->type is never XML_ELEMENT_NODE on that traversal path, so the true side stays unvisited across all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) splice and recurse on generalized token/gap structures, so grimoire can recombine whole element fragments into deeper well-formed trees. cmplog has the same I2S stage but only byte-level havoc/token mutation, so it cannot synthesize the nested element nesting; the divergence is the grimoire-structural delta, not I2S.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document containing an empty/childless element node that is immediately followed by a sibling node (or is itself empty), so that xmlTextReaderRead sets reader->state = XML_TEXTREADER_END (lines 1335 or 1374) before reaching the blocker at 1505; the false side of (reader->state != XML_TEXTREADER_END) is taken only when state == XML_TEXTREADER_END.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire-discovered winner seeds carry valid structural XML (e.g. Seed 1: '<?l ?><!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"><a><b f=""><' with nested elements). grimoire's GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators splice generalised token/gap structures, building nested element shapes that drive the reader into the END-state arms; the W-only lines 1335/1374 (reader->state = XML_TEXTREADER_END) are hit only on winner runs, and the blocker false side shows L1505 F=3 for W vs F=0 for L.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds remain in a degenerate parse (e.g. Seed 1 'wwwwwwwwwwwwml\' run of 'w' bytes prefixing the XML) that exercises the text/CDATA expansion path (L-only lines 1437-1439, xmlTextReaderExpand) instead of the empty-element-with-sibling arms; reader->state never becomes XML_TEXTREADER_END so the blocker's false side is never taken (L1505 T=26 F=0). cmplog's byte-level I2S substitution cannot synthesize the nested element-with-sibling tree structure that grimoire's structural recombination produces.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: grimoire builds on the full cmplog/I2S stack and ADDS a GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator, etc.) that splice generalised token/gap structures grammar-free. This structural recombination, absent from plain cmplog, assembles the empty-element-followed-by-sibling tree shape that drives the reader to XML_TEXTREADER_END; the single-technique delta over cmplog is precisely generalization + Grimoire mutators.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML input deep enough to reach mixed-content checking inside parsed element content while a DTD/DOCTYPE is loaded, where the current element node's name resolves through xmlGetDtdElementDesc to an elemDecl whose etype is XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED (a referenced but never-declared element). This requires a multi-construct skeleton: a valid XML prolog, a <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> external-subset reference, plus a body with character data inside an element whose name has no <!ELEMENT> declaration.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winning seeds carry a complete, structurally coherent skeleton: prolog (<?m.?>), <!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">, and a body <b>...; grimoire's generalization+structural recombination splices these token/gap structures so the parser descends through xmlParseCharData -> areBlanks -> xmlIsMixedElement with myDoc and node->name set, and the named element resolves to an UNDEFINED elemDecl, taking the true branch (W marks 3497/3498 and L3499 false; d=1 L3497 shows W T=26 F=0).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds reach the surrounding parser machinery but never satisfy the mixed-content path to the UNDEFINED case: at the blocker d=1 L3497 cmplog (L) shows T=0 F=30, and at the call site d=2 areBlanks L2921 'if (ret == 0) return(1)' the loser takes T=30 (ELEMENT_TYPE_ELEMENT, a declared element) while never producing an undeclared-element reference; cmplog's I2S byte substitution fixes scalar comparands but cannot assemble the multi-construct DTD+body skeleton that places an undeclared element name in content position.
mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire run the same shared input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so that technique is NOT the delta between them. The grimoire-only delta is the GeneralizationStage (replacing concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders) plus the Grimoire structural mutators that splice and recurse on generalised token/gap structures (string-based, grammar-free structural mutation). This grimoire structural recombination assembles the deep, multi-construct well-formed XML+DTD skeleton that reaches the mixed-element check with an undeclared element name, which the shared scalar comparison-solving stage alone (as in cmplog) cannot build.
